questions.q: Who began developing UNIX in 1969-1971, judging MULTICS too complex?
options: Ken Thompson and Dennis Ritchie (joined later by Brian Kernighan)
Richard Stallman and Linus Torvalds
Andrew Tanenbaum and Brian Kernighan
Bill Joy and Steve Bourne
questions.why: UNIX = UNiplexed Information and Computing Service, a deliberate pun on MULTICS's 'MULTiplexed'.

questions.q: Why is January 01, 1970 the reference (epoch) date of UNIX/Linux systems?
options: UNIX was developed in 1970
That is when Linux 0.02 was published
It marks the founding of Bell Labs
The C language was standardised that day
questions.why: UNIX was developed in 1970, so the start of time for UNIX/Linux systems is set at 1970-01-01.

questions.q: What language was UNIX completely rewritten in (1971), and why does that matter?
options: C - a low-level language close to the hardware, portable to any machine with a C compiler
Assembly - the fastest language available on every machine
B - the language UNIX still uses today
Fortran - the standard language of the era
questions.why: Created in Assembly, then via B to C (1971). C's portability lets the OS be adapted to any architecture with a C compiler.

questions.q: What did Richard Stallman launch in 1984?
options: The GNU (GNU's Not Unix) Project - a free and open Unix system (gcc, bash, Emacs...)
The Linux kernel
The MINIX teaching system
The Debian distribution
questions.why: GNU's own kernel, the Hurd, was started in 1990 - before Linux.

questions.q: Who developed MINIX (1987), and what for?
options: Andrew S. Tanenbaum, a simplified UNIX to teach operating systems
Linus Torvalds, as a prototype for Linux
Bell Labs, as the successor to UNIX
Richard Stallman, as the GNU kernel
questions.why: Tanenbaum made his OS's source code available - part of what a certain Finnish student built on.

options: Finnish student Linus Torvalds published version 0.02 on a Usenet discussion forum
Red Hat hired Torvalds to write a free UNIX
The GNU Project renamed the Hurd to Linux
Bell Labs open-sourced UNIX under a new name
questions.why: 'Linux' is a play on words between Linus and UNIX; other developers helped improve it.

questions.q: Match the distribution to its year: which pairing is correct?
options: Debian 1993, Red Hat 1994, Ubuntu 2004, Rocky Linux 2021
Red Hat 1991, Debian 1997, Ubuntu 2002, Rocky Linux 2018
Debian 1994, Red Hat 1993, Ubuntu 2021, Rocky Linux 2004
Debian 2002, Red Hat 2004, Ubuntu 1993, Rocky Linux 1994
questions.why: Also: KDE 1997 (Qt/C++), GNOME 1999 (GTK+), Arch 2002 (rolling release). Ubuntu is Canonical (Mark Shuttleworth), Debian-based; Rocky is based on Red Hat.

questions.q: Per the guide's info box, why do some insist on saying "GNU/Linux" rather than "Linux"?
options: Linux is strictly a kernel; the GNU project's contribution should not be forgotten
GNU wrote the Linux kernel
Linux is a trademark that cannot be used alone
GNU/Linux and Linux are entirely different operating systems
questions.why: People call the OS 'Linux' verbally, but Linux is strictly the kernel; GNU supplies much of the rest.
